title: Cross-Attention
summary: Q from one source, K/V from another. Enables connecting different data types/modalities. Score matrix is rectangular $[n_{\text{tgt}} \times n_{\text{src}}]$. K and V always come from the same source. No mask applied.
key_insights: Self-attention: Q=K=V source. Cross-attention: Q from one source, K/V from another
K and V always from SAME source (keys advertise, values deliver)
Score matrix RECTANGULAR $[n_{\text{tgt}} \times n_{\text{src}}]$ — not square
Swapping Q and K/V sources is NOT symmetric — output shape follows Q source
Pattern applies beyond language: RAG, knowledge graphs, sensor analysis

mathematical_core.KV_source: $K, V \in [B, n_{\text{src}}, d]$ from encoder/knowledge side
mathematical_core.output: Output $\in [B, n_{\text{tgt}}, d]$ — inherits Q source's sequence length

derivations.title: Asymmetry of Q vs K/V assignment

steps.name: Configuration A: Q=decoder, K/V=encoder
steps.math: $S[i,j] = Q_{\text{dec}}[i] \cdot K_{\text{enc}}[j]^\top / \sqrt{d_k}$. Output $[i] = \sum_j \text{softmax}(S[i,:])_j \cdot V_{\text{enc}}[j]$. Each decoder position retrieves a weighted blend of encoder content.

steps.name: Configuration B: Q=encoder, K/V=decoder (swapped)
steps.math: $S[i,j] = Q_{\text{enc}}[i] \cdot K_{\text{dec}}[j]^\top / \sqrt{d_k}$. Output $[i] = \sum_j \text{softmax}(S[i,:])_j \cdot V_{\text{dec}}[j]$. Each encoder position retrieves decoder content.

steps.name: Key difference
steps.math: Config A output: $[B, n_{\text{tgt}}, d]$. Config B output: $[B, n_{\text{src}}, d]$. The OUTPUT inherits the Q source's sequence length.

derivations.consequence: The Q source determines 'who is asking questions' and gets the output. The K/V source determines 'what knowledge is available'. In encoder-decoder: decoder asks, encoder provides.

derivations.title: Rectangular attention matrix properties

steps.name: Shape
steps.math: $A \in \mathbb{R}^{n_{\text{tgt}} \times n_{\text{src}}}$. Each row sums to 1 (softmax over source). Columns do NOT sum to 1.

steps.name: Column interpretation
steps.math: Column $j$: how much each target attends to source position $j$. Coverage $= \sum_i A[i,j]$. High coverage = heavily attended. Near zero = ignored.

steps.name: Coverage diagnostic
steps.math: In translation: every source word should be covered. Low coverage on position $j$ = model is missing that input.

derivations.consequence: Coverage analysis is a diagnostic tool for cross-attention quality. Missing coverage was a known issue in early seq2seq, partly solved by explicit coverage mechanisms.

derivations.title: Computational complexity comparison
derivations.math: Self-attention: $O(n^2 d_k)$ — quadratic in one sequence. Cross-attention: $O(n_{\text{tgt}} \cdot n_{\text{src}} \cdot d_k)$ — product of two lengths. If $n_{\text{tgt}} \ll n_{\text{src}}$: dominated by $n_{\text{src}}$.
derivations.consequence: For long knowledge bases with short queries, cross-attention cost is dominated by source length. This is why efficient retrieval (reducing $n_{\text{src}}$ before cross-attention) matters for RAG.

geometric_properties: Cross-attention creates an alignment manifold between two representation spaces
For translation: expect roughly diagonal pattern (with deviations for word-order changes)
Monitor: focused retrieval (peaked attention) vs broad aggregation (diffuse attention)
statistical_properties: Source coverage: $\sum_i A[i,j]$ per source position — should be non-zero for all informative positions
Attention diversity across heads: different heads should align different aspects
Cross-attention entropy: peaked = retrieval task, broad = aggregation task
numerical_properties: Memory for attention weights: $B \times h \times n_{\text{tgt}} \times n_{\text{src}}$
If $n_{\text{src}} \gg n_{\text{tgt}}$: memory dominated by source length
application_ideas: RAG: user query → Q, retrieved documents → K/V
Knowledge graph: query → Q, graph node embeddings → K/V
Hypothesis discovery: self-attention discovers patterns, cross-attention matches against references
notes: Strong intuition developed through application reasoning rather than formal study
